channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KRON4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa was nearly stationary in the central Caribbean on Friday as forecasters warned it could strengthen and swipe Jamaica as a powerful hurricane and dump a staggering amount of rain — up to 35 inches (89 centimeters) — on southwest Haiti, where they wa
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, JOHN MYERS JR. and EVENS SANON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Thieves cut through roof, steal hundreds of shoes in Florida mall heist
item.description: Florida authorities are searching for suspects who investigators said pulled off an elaborate heist to steal thousands of dollars in shoes from a Martin County mall this week.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says tech leaders and mayor changed his mind about using federal force in San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump was geared up for a show of federal force in San Francisco, a city he’s blasted as everything wrong with liberal governance. Then conversations with some of the Bay Area’s most prominent tech leaders and the mayor changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Richard Mille watches worth 'hundreds of thousands of dollars' stolen during F1 weekend
item.description: “The group targeted wealthy individuals that were wearing high-end luxury watches,” court documents said.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, NewsNation

item.title: Social Security Administration announces 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment
item.description: Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will rise 2.8 percent in 2026 after the annual cost-of-living adjustment (COLA), the federal government announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what the White House lost with the East Wing's demolition
item.description: The East Wing's razing was met with criticism both by preservationists and by over half of the American public.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, WGN 9

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, WGN 9

item.title: Alaska Airlines operations resume after ground stop over IT outage
item.description: Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it issued a temporary ground stoppage Thursday evening amid an IT outage.
item.dc:creator: Max Rego, KRON4

item.title: Alaska Airlines resumes operations after an IT outage grounded its flights for hours
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — Alaska Airlines said its operations have resumed Friday after it had to ground its planes for hours because of an information technology outage.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa to become a hurricane Saturday and rapidly intensify: NHC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — Tropical Storm Melissa is gaining strength as it remains nearly stationary near Jamaica, the National Hurricane Center said Friday. Melissa is located about 345 miles southeast of Kingston, Jamaica. Maximum sustained winds are 65 mph.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chemists in Ohio warn of rising carfentanil cases as drug mixtures grow more dangerous
item.description: Authorities across the U.S. are warning of carfentanil, a dangerous synthetic opioid 10,000 times more potent than morphine and 100 times stronger than fentanyl.
item.dc:creator: Savannah Young, WGN 9

item.title: Louisiana jury awards more than $40 million to family of man who died in privately-run jail
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A federal jury found a private company running a Louisiana jail liable for the 2015 death of a man who died of head injuries he received while in custody, and awarded the family more than $40 million in damages. Attorneys representing Erie Moore Sr.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NBA head coach and player charged in sprawling sports betting and Mafia-backed poker schemes
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers and a player for the Miami Heat were arrested Thursday along with more than 30 other people in a takedown of two sprawling gambling operations that authorities said leaked inside information about NBA athletes and rigged poker games backed
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, TIM REYNOLDS and PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia Democrats will try to reshape US House districts in counter to Trump's redistricting push
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Virginia Democrats are taking steps to redraw their state’s U.S. House districts, hoping to boost their party’s chances in next year’s midterm elections and counter President Donald Trump’s push for more partisan districts in Republican-run states.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bodycam footage of Jay Cutler's arrest released
item.description: Cutler, 41, was arrested in October 2024 after police responded to a minor traffic collision where he had rear-ended another vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, Fox 5

item.title: Nationwide auto theft ring leads to Bay Area car business: CA AG
item.description: (KRON) -- Forty felony charges were filed Thursday against eight people tied to a nationwide auto theft ring and "complex" altered vehicle identification number scheme. The suspects then sold the 19 stolen cars out of their Bay Area business, announced California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration finalizes plan to open pristine Alaska wildlife refuge to oil and gas drilling
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The Trump administration on Thursday finalized plans to open the coastal plain of Alaska’s Arctic National Wildlife Refuge to potential oil and gas drilling, renewing a long-simmering debate over whether to drill in one of the nation’s environmental jewels. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here’s how indictment says Terry Rozier shared information that paid off for bettors
item.description: NBA guard Terry Rozier was averaging nearly 21 points per game for the Charlotte Hornets as the 2022-23 season neared an end. But during a game against the New Orleans Pelicans, he scored only 5 and played less than 10 minutes before claiming he was hurt.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Eric Adams endorses once bitter rival Andrew Cuomo in New York City's mayoral race
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams endorsed Andrew Cuomo in the city’s mayoral election Thursday, throwing his weight behind a bitter rival as he cast the Democratic nominee, Zohran Mamdani, as an avatar for radicals and gentrifiers.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deadly semitrailer crash in California renews federal criticism of immigrant truck drivers
item.description: A 21-year-old semitruck driver accused of being under the influence of drugs and causing a fiery crash that killed three people on a southern California freeway is in the country illegally, U.S. Homeland Security officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs off planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco after talking to the mayor
item.description: ALAMEDA, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Thursday that he’s backing off a planned surge of federal agents into San Francisco to quell crime after speaking to the mayor and several prominent business leaders who said they're working hard to clean up the city.
item.dc:creator: TERRY CHEA and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Las Vegas connections to illegal gambling investigation, NBA fixing
item.description: An arrest was made in Las Vegas Thursday morning in connection with the FBI investigation of illegal gambling involving current and former NBA coaches and players.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a Portland protester's frog outfit
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Seth Todd was wearing an inflatable frog costume while protesting outside the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement Building in Portland, Oregon, when a federal officer unleashed a torrent of chemical spray directly into the costume's air vent. Video of the incident on Oct.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You didn't win: Mega Millions jackpot grows to $680 million
item.description: No one won big in Tuesday's drawing, and the Mega Millions jackpot has grown to an estimated $680 million.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: During cold and flu season, the youngest kids really are the germiest
item.description: Forget colorful leaves. Any caregiver knows that the real signs of fall are kids with coughs, sneezes and sniffles. Autumn marks the start of respiratory virus season, when colds, flu and other bugs start circulating — especially among the very young.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One of the world's rarest whales that makes the Atlantic its home grows in population
item.description: PORTLAND, Maine (AP) — One of the rarest whales on the planet has continued an encouraging trend of population growth in the wake of new efforts to protect the giant animals, according to scientists who study them.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court allows Trump to deploy National Guard troops to Portland
item.description: A federal appeals court on Monday said President Trump may deploy the National Guard across Oregon for now, a boon to his efforts to send troops into several Democratic-led cities.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
